contextSummary: Cloudflare's official component library. Its Select supports single and multiple selection, an items object-map/array API with per-item disabled reasons, a renderValue formatter, and JSDoc that steers hidden labels to aria-label.
rankRationale: The strongest single teaching example found: a corporate design system (Cloudflare) whose Select wrapper exercises multiple selection, the items prop as object-map or array (with per-item disabled + disabledReason descriptors), renderValue formatting, and Field-integrated labels - with JSDoc that explicitly deprecates hidden visible labels in favor of aria-label. A recomposed story would demo single vs multiple and object-map items with disabled reasons. Feeds: prop-level guidance (items, multiple), a11y contract, form integration.

contextSummary: WordPress' Block Editor monorepo. The new @wordpress/ui package wraps Base UI Select as one-file-per-part form primitives (root/trigger/item/popup/positioner/portal), pins single-select via Root<Value, false>, and documents object-value equality (Object.is, isItemEqualToValue, itemToStringLabel).
rankRationale: WordPress' new @wordpress/ui form primitives wrap Base UI Select one file per part - the cleanest anatomical decomposition in the wild - and the Root JSDoc documents object-value pitfalls (Object.is comparison, isItemEqualToValue, itemToStringLabel) while deliberately pinning single-select via Root<Value, false>. A recomposed story would show object values with items for label resolution. Feeds: anatomy, prop-level guidance (items/object values), decision-log narrative (what a mega-project standardizes).

contextSummary: AI agent framework's playground design system. Select wrapper documents its Radix-to-Base-UI migration inline: onValueChange eventDetails second arg, alignItemWithTrigger={false} to match Radix popper, and auto-derives an items map from declared SelectItems so the closed trigger can resolve labels.
rankRationale: A documented Radix-to-Base-UI migration in production code: comments explain the onValueChange eventDetails second argument, set alignItemWithTrigger={false} to preserve Radix popper behavior, and implement an items auto-derivation shim because Select.Value only resolves labels from mounted items. A recomposed story would contrast alignItemWithTrigger true/false and show the items-map fix for closed-trigger labels. Feeds: behavior (positioning/value resolution), migration guidance, pitfalls & FAQ.

contextSummary: Markdown editor for Claude & Codex. The formatting toolbar embeds Select as a block-type switcher composed via Toolbar.Button render={<Select.Trigger/>} with nativeButton={false}, controlled open state, alignItemWithTrigger={false}, and data-[starting-style] popup animation.
rankRationale: A block-type switcher inside a markdown editor's toolbar: Toolbar.Button render={<Select.Trigger/>} with nativeButton={false} is the canonical cross-component render-prop composition, plus controlled open state, alignItemWithTrigger={false}, popup-open icon rotation, and data-[starting-style] animation. Story: click the toolbar select, keyboard-navigate block types, watch the editor update. Feeds: composition handbook, animation, toolbar cluster docs.

contextSummary: graphql.org website. The site-wide theme switcher is a Base UI Select with an icon trigger, sr-only Value in compact mode, aria-label on the trigger, and align='end' positioning; wired to next-themes.
rankRationale: Theme switcher on graphql.org: an icon-trigger Select with aria-label and title on the trigger, sr-only Value in compact mode, align='end' + sideOffset positioning, wired to next-themes with a type-guarded onValueChange. Story: open the theme select, arrow through light/dark/system, verify the resolved icon swaps. Feeds: when-to-use (compact pickers), a11y contract (labelling icon triggers), real-world examples.

contextSummary: The canonical shadcn/ui registry: apps/v4/registry/bases/base is the Base UI 'base' that generates the components/ui/select.tsx wrapper found in thousands of downstream apps (16 parts, data-slot styling contract).
rankRationale: The canonical template behind thousands of hits: shadcn/ui's Base UI 'base' registry select (apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/select.tsx) covers 16 parts with the data-slot styling contract and size variants. Ranking it once explains the entire components/ui/select.tsx archetype and lets other slots go to distinctive usage. Feeds: anatomy, styling contract, ecosystem/real-world section.

contextSummary: Selia component library docs and registry. Its select wrapper uses the fullest anatomy observed in the wild - Backdrop, Arrow, ScrollUp/DownArrows, Groups, Separator - and re-exposes Positioner props (side/align/anchor/sticky/positionMethod); examples cover multiple selection and render-prop items.
rankRationale: The fullest select anatomy observed anywhere: Backdrop, Arrow, ScrollUp/DownArrows, Group/GroupLabel, Separator, plus a Popup wrapper that re-exposes Positioner props (side/align/anchor/sticky/positionMethod), and docs examples for multiple selection and render-prop items. A recomposed story is a part-by-part anatomy tour. Feeds: anatomy (all optional parts), positioning, multi-select example.

contextSummary: Questionnaire designer by INSEE (French national statistics institute). A typed options-driven form Select whose JSDoc states it is for small lists and that a Combobox should be used otherwise - live select-vs-combobox boundary evidence.
rankRationale: France's national statistics institute questionnaire designer wraps Select as a typed options-driven form control whose JSDoc states: for small lists, otherwise use a Combobox - rare first-party evidence of the select-vs-combobox decision boundary in production. Story: options-array form select with typed generic values. Feeds: when-to-use/when-not-to-use, select-vs-combobox cluster, form integration.

contextSummary: Climate Policy Radar's concept-labelling QA webapp. A prediction filter bar composes several Selects (items prop, empty-string 'All' sentinel values) with a Base UI Slider for threshold filtering.
rankRationale: A real ML-evaluation filter bar: multiple Selects with the items prop and empty-string 'All' sentinel values, composed alongside a Base UI Slider for threshold filtering, all controlled from a shared filters state. Story: set two filters and a slider, assert the filtered count updates, then clear all. Feeds: controlled usage, items prop, filter-bar archetype in real-world examples.

contextSummary: Race pace calculator site. DistanceSelect drives Next.js router navigation on selection (marathon/half/custom slugs), items prop, aria-label='Race' on a pill-shaped trigger.
rankRationale: Select as navigation: choosing a race distance pushes a Next.js route (with a 'custom' escape hatch), items prop, aria-label='Race' on a pill trigger with Value auto-resolving labels. Story: select 'Half marathon', assert URL change; select 'Custom...' for the special flow. Feeds: when-to-use boundaries (select vs menu for navigation - instructive tension), items prop guidance.

contextSummary: Community Tailwind port of KRDS, the Korean government design system. Ships a full select component plus a LanguageSwitcher built directly on Base UI Select with a Korean aria-label default and globe icon trigger.
rankRationale: Korean government design system (KRDS) Tailwind port: a dedicated LanguageSwitcher built straight on Select parts with a globe icon, Korean-language aria-label default, and label lookup from the languages array - plus a full generic select component. Story: locale picker switching document language. Feeds: i18n/locale-picker archetype, a11y labelling, gov-DS credibility for real-world section.

contextSummary: Dify, the highest-starred repo using Base UI select (147k). Its dify-ui package wraps 14 select parts with Group/GroupLabel/Separator support for the platform's workflow-builder UI.
rankRationale: The highest-profile adopter (147k stars): Dify's dify-ui package wraps 14 parts including Group/GroupLabel/Separator for its agentic-workflow platform UI. Valuable mostly as credibility and grouped-options evidence; composition is standard. Story: grouped model-picker select. Feeds: real-world examples header, Group/GroupLabel guidance.

contextSummary: AI-driven transportation management system. base-select.tsx imports the Select namespace from the @base-ui/react barrel (not the subpath), adds cva size variants and a clearable X affordance; used across enterprise admin forms.
rankRationale: Enterprise TMS forms: imports Select from the @base-ui/react barrel rather than the subpath (bundle-size teaching moment), layers cva size variants, and adds a clearable X affordance on the trigger. Story: clearable select in an admin form. Feeds: prop-level guidance (clearing value with null), import-style guidance, enterprise-form archetype.

contextSummary: Manga reader built with TanStack Start. Its select wrapper is itself composed with Base UI's useRender and mergeProps utilities - the deepest use of Base UI's composition utilities observed for select.
rankRationale: The only wrapper found that builds its select on Base UI's own composition utilities - useRender and mergeProps - showing how the library's utility layer and component layer interlock. Story: a custom-rendered trigger via useRender. Feeds: useRender/mergeProps util docs cross-link, advanced composition section.

contextSummary: AAC pictogram app for accessibility use. Settings-page LocaleSelector uses Select for language choice but hacks positioning with sideOffset={100} instead of alignItemWithTrigger={false} - an instructive misuse of the positioning API.
rankRationale: INSTRUCTIVE MISUSE: an AAC accessibility app's locale picker pushes the popup away with sideOffset={100} - almost certainly compensating for the default alignItemWithTrigger overlay behavior instead of setting alignItemWithTrigger={false}. Also renders an icon-font ligature string inside Select.Icon. The contrast story teaches the positioning model explicitly. Feeds: pitfalls & FAQ (positioning), behavior docs.

contextSummary: Typebot chatbot-builder platform. Shared UI-kit Select wraps all 16 common parts with data-slot conventions and size variants for the builder's settings surfaces.
Astrofox motion-graphics editor (Electron). Full 16-part select wrapper used for control panels that configure audio-reactive visualizations.
Privacy-first analytics platform. Shared UI select (12 parts, no List/scroll arrows) used across dashboard filters and settings.
GitHub contributions poster generator (806 stars, live). 16-part wrapper on the legacy @base-ui-components package, used for graph appearance settings.
9ui component distribution built on Base UI and Tailwind. Select docs include a dedicated multiple-selection demo, groups demo, and custom-value demo.
Prefect's generative UI framework desktop app. 15-part select wrapper (no Separator) in the renderer's UI kit.
Animated React component collection site. 16-part select among its premium-feel components.
Open-source AI calendar. 16-part select wrapper for scheduling settings.
Agentic development environment web client. 13-part select wrapper with items-prop usage.
Headless video-player UI library's docs site. 16-part select wrapper used in player settings demos (quality/speed pickers).
Cross-platform backup app. 13-part select wrapper in the shared UI library used for backup policy and schedule settings.
Minimal collaborative Postgres editor. Full 16-part select wrapper for query-console controls.
Relationship-journal PWA with an AI agent. 16-part select wrapper in shared UI.
Neobrutalism-styled component collection built on Base UI. Full 16-part select wrapper with strong visual identity.
Zebar desktop status-bar widget config. 15-part select wrapper rendered inside a native-like desktop widget environment.
Manga reading platform monorepo. 12-part select wrapper in shared UI package.
GUI for Agent Skills by devxoul. 10-part select in its UI kit.
Taiwanese civic-tech site visualizing legislators' declared wealth. 16-part select wrapper for dataset filtering.
Open-source i18n translation platform. Full 16-part select wrapper across project/language management screens.
Theming library for TanStack Start apps by a t3-oss maintainer. Example app ships a 16-part select used as the theme picker.
NieR:Automata-inspired design system. Compact 9-part select showing a minimal-but-complete composition with custom retro styling.
Aboriginal language dictionary platform. 13-part select wrapper used for dictionary/language switching.
Personal performance-focused site; select client bundle notably includes Backdrop part usage.
Official design system of Nebari (open-source data platform). Full 16-part select in its component registry.
Erasme (Lyon metropole urban lab) site. Standalone selectBox component on the legacy package.
Demo/library port by the tailwindcss-radix author exploring Base UI styling with Tailwind data-attribute variants.
Inventory demo app. GuiOption file uses only Item/ItemIndicator/ItemText - parts split across files in a decomposed custom select.
